message:
fix: Call asyncify_stop_unwind/stop_rewind exports to complete state transitions

The Asyncify runtime was missing two critical export calls from the
binaryen asyncify protocol:

1. asyncify_stop_unwind() — must be called after fn() returns during
   unwind to finalize stack saving. Without this, the asyncify state
   machine remained in UNWINDING state indefinitely.

2. asyncify_stop_rewind() — must be called in the wrapped import when
   re-entered during rewind, before returning the cached value. Without
   this, the rewind was never finalized.

The code incorrectly relied on an "asyncify" import namespace (WASM
calling JS), but wasm-opt --asyncify only adds exports, not imports.
Those import handlers were never invoked, leaving the state machine
broken for every async host import (KV, R2, D1, HTTP, TCP).

This caused "CompileError: unreachable" / "RuntimeError: unreachable"
on any async host import call, while sync imports (env_get) worked fine.
